objective: Repair agent preflight so an explicitly requested corrective rerun can claim an already accepted terminal batch without weakening the default closed-issue or delivery-governance gates.
claim_ceiling: Registration only: no code, batch transition, issue reopening, source call, credential access, runtime activation, scheduler, release binding, or Production data claim.

requirements: queued registration is merged on main before a preparation revision pins the activation base and reviewer
the implementation accepts only an explicit opt-in for the same closed issue whose canonical batch is terminal done with one valid matching accepted, target, and terminal rung
the implementation preserves clean-worktree, upstream, duplicate-claim, exact-work-key, and delivery-governance checks
focused tests prove default rejection and every closed-batch rejection case before E0 acceptance

later_claim_dependencies: D4 may use a future accepted D9 E0 result only to claim its existing checker-authorized done-to-done corrective terminal rerun; D9 does not authorize a D4 handoff or any D8 implementation.
D8 remains queued until all of its own accepted interface, persistence, provenance, Staging, stable-read, release/recovery, source-rights, and SLO prerequisites are satisfied.

corpus.environment: Local/CI preflight fixtures and a closed terminal D4 manifest only
required_strata: an open standalone issue remains claimable under the default path
a closed issue is rejected by default
an explicit opt-in accepts only a closed terminal done batch with matching valid rungs
closed standalone, non-terminal, malformed, or mismatched-rung batches are rejected
duplicate work claims and dirty worktrees remain rejected

source_ceiling: No network source, credential, Vault, database, object-store, scheduler, runtime, release, or Production data operation is authorized.

release_activation.reason: D9 only hardens local agent claim validation and cannot activate a runtime, source, schedule, consumer, release, or data flow.

negative_controls: any closed issue claim without the explicit terminal-corrective option
a closed standalone issue, a missing/malformed batch manifest, a non-terminal batch, or mismatched/invalid rungs
bypassing clean-worktree, upstream, duplicate-claim, exact-work-key, or governance validation
reopening D4, changing D4's accepted interface, or authorizing D8, source, scheduler, runtime, release, or Production data work

allowed_evidence_readers: issue #274 planning only until a later accepted handoff names a consumer

invalidation: Changing the allowed closed-issue condition, rung vocabulary, manifest selection, duplicate-claim behavior, or any preserved preflight gate requires a new prepared revision before implementation.
Any attempt to combine this helper with a D4 permission change, D8 implementation, source access, scheduler, runtime, release, or data path requires separate authorization.
rollback: Revert only the future D9 preflight helper and its evidence; do not mutate D4, D8, issue lifecycle state, source policy, runtime, or historical governance evidence.
